text.title: gce-managed-instance-groups Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gce-managed-instance-groups. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Production MIGs MUST be regional (not zonal), MUST set minimum instances to at least 3 (one per zone), and MUST configure distribution_policy_target_shape to EVEN. Zonal MIGs are permitted only for dev/test environments.
rule.rationale: A zonal MIG becomes fully unavailable during a zone outage. Regional MIGs with EVEN distribution ensure capacity across all three zones; a zone outage reduces capacity to 66% rather than 0%.

rule.statement: Every production MIG MUST configure autohealing with an HTTP(S) health check and set initial_delay_sec to at least the full VM startup plus application warmup time. The health check path MUST return HTTP 200 only when the application is fully ready to serve traffic.
rule.rationale: If initial_delay_sec is too short, the autoheal loop replaces healthy VMs that are still starting, causing a thrashing loop that takes the entire MIG offline. The health check path must reflect true readiness, not just that the process is running.

rule.statement: Rolling updates on production MIGs MUST set max_unavailable_fixed = 0 (or maxUnavailable=0) and max_surge_fixed >= 1 to ensure capacity is never reduced during the update. Update type MUST be PROACTIVE so instances are replaced immediately rather than waiting for natural turnover.
rule.rationale: maxUnavailable=0 guarantees that the load balancer always has the full target pool available. maxSurge>=1 provides the surge capacity needed to start new-template instances before draining old ones. PROACTIVE mode ensures the update actually completes; OPPORTUNISTIC only replaces on recreation events and can leave the MIG in a mixed state indefinitely.
